name: ExtensionSupplyDelivery_SuppliedItem
title: R5: suppliedItem
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` 0..* `BackboneElement`
*  R4B: `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` 0..1 `BackboneElement`
*  R4: `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` 0..1 `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.comment:
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.quantity` is part of an existing definition because parent element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.quantity` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.quantity` as `Equivalent`.
element.requirements:
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.quantity` is part of an existing definition because parent element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.quantity` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.quantity` as `Equivalent`.

element.definition: The amount of the item that has been supplied.  Unit of measure may be included.

element.short: R5: Medication, Substance, Device or Biologically Derived Product supplied
element.comment:
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` is part of an existing definition because parent element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` with unmapped reference targets: BiologicallyDerivedProduct, InventoryItem, NutritionProduct.
Source element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.
element.requirements:
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` is part of an existing definition because parent element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The standard extension `alternate-reference` has been mapped as the representation of FHIR R5 element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` with unmapped reference targets: BiologicallyDerivedProduct, InventoryItem, NutritionProduct.
Source element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` has unmapped reference types. While the target element `SupplyDelivery.suppliedItem.item[x]` is a choice type and does not allow extensions, the `alternate-reference` extension can be applied to Reference values within it.

element.short: Medication, Substance, Device or Biologically Derived Product supplied
element.definition: Identifies the medication, substance, device or biologically derived product being supplied. This is either a link to a resource representing the details of the item or a code that identifies the item from a known list.
